differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus with lupus nephritis and secondary antiphospholipid syndrome
next_steps: Serologic workup including ANA, anti-dsDNA, C3, C4, anti-phospholipid antibodies (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta2 glycoprotein I)
Perform renal biopsy to characterize the nature of the nephropathy
Evaluate for systemic inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP)
Perform echocardiogram to evaluate for cardiac thrombus or vegetations
Consider early administration of high-dose corticosteroids once infection is controlled, pending biopsy results

differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) with lupus nephritis (Class V) and secondary Antiphospholipid Syndrome
Membranous Nephropathy with secondary venous thromboembolism
Infectious endocarditis with systemic septic emboli and immune-complex glomerulonephritis
ANCA-associated vasculitis with secondary renal vein thrombosis
next_steps: Obtain definitive autoimmune serologies: ANA, anti-dsDNA, ENA panel, C3, C4, and anti-phospholipid antibody panel (Lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta2-GPI)
Perform transesophageal echocardiography to rule out endocarditis or intracardiac thrombus
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics pending final culture results given high fever and pulmonary consolidations
Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation with parenteral heparin while monitoring renal function and bleeding risk
Assess for hemolytic anemia by checking LDH, haptoglobin, peripheral blood smear, and reticulocyte count
Perform renal biopsy to determine the primary pathology (e.g., membranous pattern, proliferative GN) and assess chronicity
Measure serum levels of antithrombin III, protein C, and protein S to assess for hypercoagulable states associated with nephrotic syndrome
